title: Visualization of Interior, Boundary, and Exterior Points in a Subset of a Metric Space
description: A Venn diagram style illustration showing a universal set X (the metric space) containing a subset A. The diagram highlights the classification of points based on their neighborhoods relative to A, defining interior, boundary, and exterior points.
elements: The universal set X (representing the entire metric space)
A subset A of X (an arbitrary set within the space)
An interior point of A with its open ball/nbd contained in A
A boundary point of A with its open ball intersecting both A and its complement
An exterior point of A with its open ball contained in the complement of A
relationships: The set A is a proper subset of X
The exterior of A is defined as the interior of the complement of A
The boundary of A is the set of points that are neither in the interior nor the exterior of A
The open balls (neighborhoods) of points demonstrate the definitions: entirely inside A (interior), overlapping both A and not-A (boundary), or entirely outside A (exterior)
